title: Traffic Control SDK: API contracts for canary, A/B, shadowing and auto-rollback
preview: Define and finalize the public API and SDK client helper contracts for traffic control use-cases: canary rollouts, A/B tests, shadow traffic, and automated rollback. This ticket scopes server endpoints, client-side idempotency and retry semantics, RBAC/permission surfaces, audit logging expectations, and the metric/...
text: Define and finalize the public API and SDK client helper contracts for traffic control use-cases: canary rollouts, A/B tests, shadow traffic, and automated rollback. This ticket scopes server endpoints, client-side idempotency and retry semantics, RBAC/permission surfaces, audit logging expectations, and the metric/signals model used to trigger auto-rollback decisions. Goal is a stable contract that can be implemented by engineering and consumed by internal platform teams and external SDK users.

title: Q3 Synced Roadmap: Hosted API Feature Phasing & Model Catalog Convergence
preview: Quarterly planning ticket to align Hosted API feature phases with model catalog transitions for Q3. Objective is to produce a sequenced rollout plan that minimizes runtime risk, keeps SLAs intact, and preserves predictable unit economics while enabling new model variants in the catalog. This ticket coordinates cross...
text: Quarterly planning ticket to align Hosted API feature phases with model catalog transitions for Q3. Objective is to produce a sequenced rollout plan that minimizes runtime risk, keeps SLAs intact, and preserves predictable unit economics while enabling new model variants in the catalog. This ticket coordinates cross-team decisions (runtime, infra, design, sales) and produces the final phased milestone plan for Product and Eng to execute. Scope includes feature gating, phasing of model variants, compatibility guardrails, customer migration guidance, and OKR mapping. Out of scope: Dedicated and Private-specific rollout mechanics (handled in separate tickets).

title: Hybrid-reserved GPU fit & SLA triage playbook for Dedicated intake
preview: Summary: Create an operational playbook and intake rubric to evaluate enterprise requests for Dedicated capacity that combine reserved throughput with limited autoscaling/burst (hybrid-reserved). This ticket defines the decision flow for SLA targets, reserved throughput sizing, GPU class selection, burst credit hand...
text:
Summary: Create an operational playbook and intake rubric to evaluate enterprise requests for Dedicated capacity that combine reserved throughput with limited autoscaling/burst (hybrid-reserved). This ticket defines the decision flow for SLA targets, reserved throughput sizing, GPU class selection, burst credit handling, and required preflight tests. Goal is to reduce time-to-offer for sales/solutions and ensure engineering can validate risk and operational cost before commitments.

Scope:
- Intake questionnaire (customer-facing) and internal scoring rubric (cost sensitivity, latency SLA, availability SLA, KV cache locality needs, model variants).
- GPU fit decision matrix that weighs model family, average sequence length, KV cache locality, and requested reserved throughput to recommend GPU class and quantization profile.
- Burst policy & preflight: define burst credit pools, probe j...

title: Adaptive alert routing + sticky dedupe for evals regressions triggered by config/model changes
preview: Problem: Customers and SREs receive noisy eval alerts when model or config changes cause transient drops in eval signals. Current Console alerting can produce duplicates across routes (Slack channels, PagerDuty services) and lacks consistent grouping when the same regression is observed across related eval sets or r...
text:
Problem: Customers and SREs receive noisy eval alerts when model or config changes cause transient drops in eval signals. Current Console alerting can produce duplicates across routes (Slack channels, PagerDuty services) and lacks consistent grouping when the same regression is observed across related eval sets or rollout steps.

Goal: Add an adaptive routing and deduplication layer in Console that ties eval regression alerts to the causative change (model_version, config_hash, rollout_id) and routes a single, grouped alert to the correct on-call or owner with optional fanout. The solution should: (1) reduce noisy duplicates, (2) preserve fast detection and paging for real regressions, (3) be configurable per-team and per-project, and (4) integrate with existing Slack and PagerDuty destinations and our rollout/canary metadata.

Proposed solution:
- Sticky dedupe key: construct a deter...

title: Private deployment security contracts: SSO/RBAC/KMS/audit acceptance & signoff
preview: PRD-level acceptance contract for private (VPC / on-prem) customer onboarding: consolidate interface-level requirements for SSO (SAML/OIDC), RBAC semantics, audit log schema & retention, and KMS/HSM integration. Purpose is to create a deterministic signoff checklist and evidence matrix so Sales/CS/Onboarding enginee...
text: PRD-level acceptance contract for private (VPC / on-prem) customer onboarding: consolidate interface-level requirements for SSO (SAML/OIDC), RBAC semantics, audit log schema & retention, and KMS/HSM integration. Purpose is to create a deterministic signoff checklist and evidence matrix so Sales/CS/Onboarding engineers can validate compliance needs up front and reduce rework during enterprise onboarding.

title: Progressive upgrade orchestration: customer migration roadmap and adaptive comms kit
preview:
Purpose:
This ticket defines a reusable, progressive customer upgrade playbook and a set of adaptive communication templates tailored to Redwood's LTS / stable channel strategy. The goal is to give account teams and automated tooling a deterministic path for migrating customers across minor/major versions with clear...
text:
Purpose:
This ticket defines a reusable, progressive customer upgrade playbook and a set of adaptive communication templates tailored to Redwood's LTS / stable channel strategy. The goal is to give account teams and automated tooling a deterministic path for migrating customers across minor/major versions with clear rollout gates, measurable canary success criteria, and templated external comms (email, console banner, changelog, migration guide).

Scope and non-goals:
- In scope: customer-facing artifacts (changelogs, targeted migration emails, staged console banners), gating criteria for automatic/assisted upgrades, SDK compatibility mapping, example rollback messaging, and links to implementation tasks for automation.
- Out of scope: runtime binary build engineering, CI job creation (ENG will own), and legal acceptance for contract changes.

Key principles:
1) Minimize customer surp...

title: QBR Board Prep: Technical Risk Heatmap + Investment Asks
preview: Purpose: Prepare a concise technical-risk heatmap and associated investment asks for the upcoming quarterly business review (QBR) and board packet. Goal is to surface the top 6 technical risks that could materially affect our Q2 SLAs, capacity commitments for Dedicated customers, and Private deployment stability, an...
text:
Purpose: Prepare a concise technical-risk heatmap and associated investment asks for the upcoming quarterly business review (QBR) and board packet. Goal is to surface the top 6 technical risks that could materially affect our Q2 SLAs, capacity commitments for Dedicated customers, and Private deployment stability, and to recommend budget/CAPEX or headcount asks with clear decision criteria.

Scope: Analysis will cover serving runtime risks (batching/KV cache regressions), capacity limits and burst behavior for Dedicated, Private upgrade/rollback risk, operational observability gaps that increase MTTR, and model-compatibility / kernel support gaps that could force fallbacks. Excludes product roadmap feature asks not directly tied to risk reduction.

Deliverables: (1) A one-page heatmap slide (likelihood vs impact) with 6 prioritized risks and mitigations; (2) Two-slide investment ask de...

title: MSA QuickPass: Automated low-risk redline approvals with pricing delta flagging
preview: Build an automated QuickPass workflow for Master Service Agreement (MSA) and accompanying DPA redlines that allows low-risk requests to be approved without full legal review, while surfacing a pricing delta and audit trail to Sales and Finance. The flow should integrate security questionnaire scores, indemnity/liabi...
text: Build an automated QuickPass workflow for Master Service Agreement (MSA) and accompanying DPA redlines that allows low-risk requests to be approved without full legal review, while surfacing a pricing delta and audit trail to Sales and Finance. The flow should integrate security questionnaire scores, indemnity/liability caps thresholds, and a simple UI in the quote screen that shows the required price uplift if the customer requests concessions outside the QuickPass envelope. This reduces cycle time for low-risk customers and lets us run pricing/packaging experiments to measure revenue impact of accepting small liability increases vs. rejecting or escalating.

title: Model Bundling Economics & Regional Substitution Simulations
preview: Objective: Determine optimal productized bundles and procurement trigger rules that prioritize latency-sensitive customers while minimizing expected unit cost across regions. We will synthesize telemetry and market pricing to simulate realistic substitution events (model provider swaps, quantization fallbacks, regio...
text:
Objective: Determine optimal productized bundles and procurement trigger rules that prioritize latency-sensitive customers while minimizing expected unit cost across regions. We will synthesize telemetry and market pricing to simulate realistic substitution events (model provider swaps, quantization fallbacks, regional outages) and produce a recommended tiered bundling strategy (latency-first, cost-first, balanced) plus procurement escalation criteria for Dedicated/Private sales.

Scope:
- Competitive scan of bundled offers (managed model bundles, reserved GPU + hosting credits, enterprise quant bundles) across 6 vendors in NA/EU/APAC.
- Build Monte Carlo substitution simulator that ingests: per-model token cost, per-region median/95th latency distributions, quantization quality penalties (per workload class), auto-batching impact curves, and SLA rollback probabilities.
- Run simulati...

title: Sprint-to-strategy translation kit and exec briefing playbook
preview: Purpose: Create a repeatable 'translation kit' and playbook that converts sprint-level metrics, qualitative signals, and open risks into a concise executive narrative for monthly executive readouts, QBRs, and board packets. Background: PMs and Eng leads currently handcraft slides for each readout; this is time-consu...
text: Purpose: Create a repeatable 'translation kit' and playbook that converts sprint-level metrics, qualitative signals, and open risks into a concise executive narrative for monthly executive readouts, QBRs, and board packets. Background: PMs and Eng leads currently handcraft slides for each readout; this is time-consuming and inconsistently framed which makes cross-initiative comparison hard. Goal: reduce time to prepare an exec-ready one-pager from ~8 hours to <2 hours and to standardize signals used to drive OKR updates and investment asks.

title: Hybrid-Minute Reservation Bands, Soft-Preempt Policy, and SLA Tier Pilot
preview:
Context: Enterprise customers request more granular reserved capacity options to better match variable workloads (short bursts and minute-level spikes) without paying for long-duration commitments. Current committed-hour SKU is too coarse for many ML infra patterns (jobs with 1-30 minute peaks).

Hypothesis: Introdu...
text:
Context: Enterprise customers request more granular reserved capacity options to better match variable workloads (short bursts and minute-level spikes) without paying for long-duration commitments. Current committed-hour SKU is too coarse for many ML infra patterns (jobs with 1-30 minute peaks).

Hypothesis: Introducing minute-level reservation bands (e.g., 15, 60, 180 minute commit bands) combined with a soft-preempt policy and a small overage credit pool will increase conversion from trial to committed customers by 8-12% among mid-market accounts and reduce short-term overprovision requests, while preserving predictable revenue.

Proposal: Run a 12-week pilot offering a new SKU family: Hybrid-Minute. Key elements:
- Reservation bands sold as bundles of guaranteed-minutes per month (e.g., 10k x 15m blocks, 4k x 60m blocks).
- Soft-preempt policy: when a reserved minute is consumed, t...

title: Stratified prompt sampling and actionable regression contracts for Optimize evals
preview: Define a reproducible, actionable evaluation contract for Optimize that ties prompt-set sampling, regression thresholds, and A/B decision rules to concrete rollout/rollback actions. The objective is to make eval outputs prescriptive (what to do) rather than descriptive (what happened). This ticket consolidates requi...
text: Define a reproducible, actionable evaluation contract for Optimize that ties prompt-set sampling, regression thresholds, and A/B decision rules to concrete rollout/rollback actions. The objective is to make eval outputs prescriptive (what to do) rather than descriptive (what happened). This ticket consolidates requirements for sampling stratification, evidence thresholds, stop/rollback logic, monitoring signals, and handoff to SRE/eng for automations.

title: Adaptive Quarter Plan: Cohort-based Hosted Endpoint Phasing & Model Retirement
preview: Quarterly plan to phase new hosted endpoint offerings by customer cohort, schedule model retirement windows for older variants, and define canary/rollback policies. Focus is on minimizing customer churn during lineup rotations while preserving capacity headroom and meeting latency SLOs. Plan spans product decisions,...
text: Quarterly plan to phase new hosted endpoint offerings by customer cohort, schedule model retirement windows for older variants, and define canary/rollback policies. Focus is on minimizing customer churn during lineup rotations while preserving capacity headroom and meeting latency SLOs. Plan spans product decisions, capacity allocations, canary cadence, and observability signals for automated rollback.

title: Update PRD to convert reliability postmortem actions into runtime fallback requirements
preview: Background: During the Feb 24th production incident (INC-2026-02-24-kv-eviction), a cascade of events led to a sustained increase in request errors and tail latency for certain conversational workloads. Root causes identified in the postmortem: (1) an aggressive KV cache eviction policy that caused frequent cache mi...
text:
Background: During the Feb 24th production incident (INC-2026-02-24-kv-eviction), a cascade of events led to a sustained increase in request errors and tail latency for certain conversational workloads. Root causes identified in the postmortem: (1) an aggressive KV cache eviction policy that caused frequent cache misses for long-session customers, (2) routing logic failed to select a compatible fallback model variant when the primary GPU pool had transient memory exhaustion, and (3) missing high-priority alerts for fallback activation and error budget burn.

Goal: Translate the postmortem action items into explicit PRD updates so that engineering has clear acceptance criteria to implement: robust runtime fallback behavior, deterministic compatibility rules for model variants, improved signals/alerts, and rollout/rollback guards tied to SLOs and customer-impact thresholds.

Scope: This...

title: Tenant audit snapshot generator with PDF signing and CSV normalization
preview: Problem: Customers need audit-ready exports that combine residency-aware data selection, retention-safe redaction, and deterministic file formats (PDF with verifiable signing and canonical CSV rows) delivered on configurable schedules. Current export options are ad-hoc, produce inconsistent CSV column orders, and do...
text:
Problem: Customers need audit-ready exports that combine residency-aware data selection, retention-safe redaction, and deterministic file formats (PDF with verifiable signing and canonical CSV rows) delivered on configurable schedules. Current export options are ad-hoc, produce inconsistent CSV column orders, and do not include a signed manifest that proves file provenance for audits.

Goal: Build a tenant-scoped snapshot generator that produces a packaged export per tenant containing: (1) a signed PDF report with table-of-contents and tenant-safe redactions; (2) canonicalized CSV datasets with stable column ordering and typed schema headers; (3) a machine-verifiable manifest and hash list (SHA-256) plus optional PKI-style signature for enterprise customers. Exports must respect regional residency filters and retention rules, and be deliverable via pre-signed S3 links and email webhoo...

title: Milestone cadence, scope-lockdown windows, and emergency rollback runway for API / Console / Runtime
preview: Goal: produce a single, actionable milestone cadence and a clear scope-lockdown + rollback runway tailored to the three-component release (API, Console, Runtime). Deliverables: (1) a published milestone calendar with freeze windows and owners, (2) a living cut-criteria matrix that maps severity -> allowed scope, (3)...
text: Goal: produce a single, actionable milestone cadence and a clear scope-lockdown + rollback runway tailored to the three-component release (API, Console, Runtime). Deliverables: (1) a published milestone calendar with freeze windows and owners, (2) a living cut-criteria matrix that maps severity -> allowed scope, (3) an emergency rollback runbook and Canary windows schedule, and (4) a release-notes coordination plan and templates for PM/Eng/Design/GTM.

title: Segmented Slab Reserves and Proportional Burst Allocation (PRD)
preview:
Overview:
This PRD proposes a new allocation model for Dedicated capacity called 'Segmented Slab Reserves' (SSRs) paired with a Proportional Burst Allocation (PBA) mechanism. SSRs divide a tenant's reserved committed capacity into multiple fixed-size slabs that can be independently used, released, or prewarmed. PBA ...
text:
Overview:
This PRD proposes a new allocation model for Dedicated capacity called 'Segmented Slab Reserves' (SSRs) paired with a Proportional Burst Allocation (PBA) mechanism. SSRs divide a tenant's reserved committed capacity into multiple fixed-size slabs that can be independently used, released, or prewarmed. PBA provides a bounded, auditable burst runway that draws proportional credits from a global burst pool and decays over time.

Motivation and problem statement:
Current reserved-slot models are coarse-grained (single contiguous reservation per tenant) which causes: wasted headroom on uneven load patterns, poor isolation when tenants spike, and brittle autoscaling when combined with short-lived bursts. Dedicated customers want: predictable baseline throughput, controlled temporary burst above baseline, and transparent accounting for burst usage. Operators need easier reclamation...
links:
- Design mockups: drive.google.com/example-ssr-mocks
- Draft API spec: github.com/redwood-inference/dedicated-api/pull/812
- Operator runbook template: confluence.redwood.ai/dedicated-slab-runbook
- Pilot sign-up sheet: docs.google.com/forms/example-pilot
- Dependency ENG-4820: prewarm orchestration service design doc
- Billing schema PR: github.com/redwood-inference/billing/pull/49

title: Probabilistic fallback prioritization & experiment roadmap for model availability
preview: Objective: build a defensible, data-driven playbook and A/B experiment roadmap that ranks candidate fallback models probabilistically (probability of acceptable output) given observed signals from the model landscape and customer workload patterns. The playbook's goal is to minimize customer-visible regressions whil...
text:
Objective: build a defensible, data-driven playbook and A/B experiment roadmap that ranks candidate fallback models probabilistically (probability of acceptable output) given observed signals from the model landscape and customer workload patterns. The playbook's goal is to minimize customer-visible regressions while optimizing token cost and latency across Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployments.

Context: customers expect graceful degradation when primary models are throttled, change, or become unavailable. Current fallback heuristics are ad hoc (static prioritization by latency then cost). Market research and competitive scans show rivals exposing model variant guarantees and automated fallback tiers; we need a formalized approach that ties competitive signals, regional availability, quantization compatibility, and empirical quality regressions into a single prioritization engine.

title: Access request orchestration & triage protocol for enterprise admin features
preview: Create a repeatable orchestration and prioritization protocol for non-bug admin & access management requests (RBAC policies, SSO/SAML onboarding, SCIM provisioning, org-level policy adjustments). Goal: reduce manual PM engineering routing, ensure high-risk enterprise requests are fast-tracked, and produce a signal-t...
text: Create a repeatable orchestration and prioritization protocol for non-bug admin & access management requests (RBAC policies, SSO/SAML onboarding, SCIM provisioning, org-level policy adjustments). Goal: reduce manual PM engineering routing, ensure high-risk enterprise requests are fast-tracked, and produce a signal-to-priority mapping that can be automated in the intake layer.

title: Staffing Mosaic & Graceful Trim Playbook for Cycle Allocation
preview: Objective: Create a pragmatic, ops-friendly playbook that helps PMs and Eng managers size a cycle by composing a "mosaic" of staffing slices (core commits, surge lanes, on-call buffer, and cross-functional steady-state) and a lightweight graceful-trim decision flow for mid-cycle scope cuts. The playbook should be ac...
text: Objective: Create a pragmatic, ops-friendly playbook that helps PMs and Eng managers size a cycle by composing a "mosaic" of staffing slices (core commits, surge lanes, on-call buffer, and cross-functional steady-state) and a lightweight graceful-trim decision flow for mid-cycle scope cuts. The playbook should be actionable in a 30–60 minute triage workshop, integrate with existing OKR priorities, and produce a prioritized scope list plus a staffing allocation sheet that can be consumed by the Capacity Board and CTO review. Deliverables: 1) One-page staffing mosaic template (CSV + Google Sheet), 2) Graceful Trim decision flow (checklist + scoring matrix), 3) Example run through for two sample initiatives (runtime perf and console UX), 4) Templates for Slack/Notion update and approval workflow. Out of scope: replacing formal resource-allocation policy; this is a tactical triage/playboo...

title: Reserve Safelist and Burst Scheduler for Dedicated Capacity
preview: Problem: Customers on Dedicated capacity sometimes experience noisy-neighbor interference during transient bursts. Current reserved pool semantics provide hard reservations and vague soft-burst behavior, but we lack a lightweight mechanism to guarantee minimal forward progress for a small group of mission-critical t...
text:
Problem: Customers on Dedicated capacity sometimes experience noisy-neighbor interference during transient bursts. Current reserved pool semantics provide hard reservations and vague soft-burst behavior, but we lack a lightweight mechanism to guarantee minimal forward progress for a small group of mission-critical tenants while still allowing elastic burst traffic.

This PRD proposes two coordinated features: (1) a Reserve Safelist: a configurable micro-reservation that guarantees a tiny, constantly-available slice of capacity for nominated tenants (useful for latency-sensitive endpoints and compliance workloads); and (2) a Burst Scheduler: a credit-based short-window scheduler that fairly mediates transient burst traffic across tenants, integrates with existing reserved pools and autoscaling triggers, and supports graceful preemption and cooldown windows. The combined goal is to impr...

title: Inbound open-model risk bucketing and staged rollout contract
preview: Clarify requirements for how new open (community) model submissions are classified by risk, what metadata gates they must include, and the rollout contract that binds pinning, telemetry gating, and staged percentage exposure. This PRD fragment is intended to: 1) define discrete risk buckets (Low/Moderate/High) with ...
text: Clarify requirements for how new open (community) model submissions are classified by risk, what metadata gates they must include, and the rollout contract that binds pinning, telemetry gating, and staged percentage exposure. This PRD fragment is intended to: 1) define discrete risk buckets (Low/Moderate/High) with concrete signal thresholds; 2) specify minimum intake metadata required to accept an unpinned variant; 3) prescribe a staged rollout plan with telemetry gates and automatic rollback triggers; and 4) document operator responsibilities and handoffs to SRE/security for final admission. Goal is to give engineering clear pass/fail criteria for the intake pipeline and a deterministic rollout contract for Catalog and Runtime teams.

title: Client onramp prioritization rubric — language coverage, auth patterns & streaming ergonomics
preview: Problem: We receive a steady stream of customer requests for new language client libraries and auth/streaming ergonomics changes. Without a consistent intake and prioritization rubric we over-index on vocal requests and create engineering churn. This ticket defines a repeatable rubric and intake flow for triaging SD...
text:
Problem: We receive a steady stream of customer requests for new language client libraries and auth/streaming ergonomics changes. Without a consistent intake and prioritization rubric we over-index on vocal requests and create engineering churn. This ticket defines a repeatable rubric and intake flow for triaging SDK/client requests with a focus on enterprise needs (auth patterns, token rotation, SSO/OIDC, resumable streaming, backpressure) and language coverage trade-offs.

Goal: Establish a lightweight scoring rubric, an intake form, and a small gating checklist so PM/Eng/Design can rapidly decide: Build native SDK, provide official adapter, or document a community pattern. Deliverables:
- Prioritization rubric (weighted scores and thresholds)
- Intake form template for Solutions/Support/AE to capture signals
- Triage runbook with owners and SLAs (48h acknowledge, 10 business days i...

title: Org-scoped adaptive regression pipelines with encrypted shadow probes
preview: Problem: Customers running Dedicated/Private deployments require regression detection tightly coupled to model/config changes, but cannot allow customer data to leave their tenancy. We need a lightweight, org-scoped evaluation orchestration that runs privacy-preserving probes/shadow tests whenever model config or ro...
text:
Problem: Customers running Dedicated/Private deployments require regression detection tightly coupled to model/config changes, but cannot allow customer data to leave their tenancy. We need a lightweight, org-scoped evaluation orchestration that runs privacy-preserving probes/shadow tests whenever model config or routing policies change.

Goal: Deliver an adaptive per-organization eval pipeline that: 1) triggers on model/config rollouts and routing policy changes; 2) executes inside the customer's dedicated environment (VPC/on-prem) or via client-side sampling; 3) stores only encrypted witnesses/hashes and metric aggregates in Redwood control plane; 4) provides actionable regression alerts and an opt-in automated rollback/canary flow.

Approach: Build a small runner SDK that can be deployed into Private/Dedicated tenants (container + minimal runtime) to execute curated probe suites. F...

title: Account-led feature curation and scoping-sparring play
preview: This ticket defines a repeatable play for aggregating account-sourced feature asks from Sales and Customer Success and producing a lightweight, scoping-grade output that can drive prioritization decisions. The goal is to reduce back-and-forth with Sales/CS by providing a standardized scoring and sparring session (30...
text: This ticket defines a repeatable play for aggregating account-sourced feature asks from Sales and Customer Success and producing a lightweight, scoping-grade output that can drive prioritization decisions. The goal is to reduce back-and-forth with Sales/CS by providing a standardized scoring and sparring session (30- to 60-minute) where PM + 1 engineer + AE validate business intent, basic technical feasibility, privacy/regulatory constraints, and an initial effort bucket. The artifact delivered is an "account bundle brief" (single-page) and a normalized scoring row in the enterprise intake board.

title: PRD: Adversarial prompt probes, cohorted signal attenuation, and circuit-breaker rollback for Optimize
preview: Goal: Add an automated layer to Optimize that (1) generates adversarial probe prompts to stress test model variants, (2) groups signals into stable cohorts with attenuation for noisy indicators, and (3) enforces a two-tier circuit-breaker rollback choreography (soft-stop then hard-stop) tied to composite regression ...
text:
Goal: Add an automated layer to Optimize that (1) generates adversarial probe prompts to stress test model variants, (2) groups signals into stable cohorts with attenuation for noisy indicators, and (3) enforces a two-tier circuit-breaker rollback choreography (soft-stop then hard-stop) tied to composite regression scoring. This PRD specifies requirements, acceptance criteria, and rollout steps for the 2026-Q2 Optimize quality-safety initiative.

Background: Current Optimize evals rely on static promptsets and fixed thresholds that underreact to adversarial or distribution-shift events. Customers running production can experience silent regressions in small but important cohorts (e.g., legal, finance, medical). We need a system that proactively finds brittle prompts, weights cohort signals by reliability, and automates safe traffic decay/rollback when multiple signals align.

Non-goal...

title: Establish review runbook and handshake protocol for cross-team PRD approvals
preview: Background: Recent PRDs for Dedicated autoscale and Private upgrade experienced multi-week delays due to unclear reviewer responsibilities, missing legal checklist items, and inconsistent handoffs between PM, Design, and Engineering. Goal: define a lightweight, repeatable runbook and ’handshake’ protocol that standa...
text: Background: Recent PRDs for Dedicated autoscale and Private upgrade experienced multi-week delays due to unclear reviewer responsibilities, missing legal checklist items, and inconsistent handoffs between PM, Design, and Engineering. Goal: define a lightweight, repeatable runbook and ’handshake’ protocol that standardizes how PRDs move through review and sign-off with SLAs, templates, and an escalation path. Deliverables include a Confluence runbook, a PRD template with section-level owners and checkboxes, a Linear review workflow pattern, and a pilot with two upcoming PRDs (Dedicated autoscale and Private upgrades).

title: Coordinated Sunset Workflow: Automated Notices, Pin Recommendations, and Rollback Paths for Legacy Model Versions
preview:
Goal: Define and deliver an automated, low-friction sunset workflow for aging model versions that combines proactive customer notices, in-console pin recommendations, published performance/compatibility profiles, and safe rollback paths.

Background: We operate many model variants and publish performance profiles fo...
text:
Goal: Define and deliver an automated, low-friction sunset workflow for aging model versions that combines proactive customer notices, in-console pin recommendations, published performance/compatibility profiles, and safe rollback paths.

Background: We operate many model variants and publish performance profiles for each. Customers sometimes rely on older weights or older quantized builds. When we must retire a version (weight or tuning), ad-hoc notices and manual support create churn and escalations. This initiative packages the end-to-end flow so we can retire versions with predictable customer outcomes and minimal support load.

Key deliverables: 1) Sunset timeline template with notice windows (early warning, last-call, final shutdown) and per-tier variations (self-serve, enterprise SLA). 2) In-console "Pin Recommendation" card: automatically suggests compatible stable variants an...

title: Coordinate cross-team acceptance matrix for Private VPC upgrade toggle
preview: PRD: Add a user-facing toggle in the Redwood Console to enable the Private VPC upgrade path (v2 networking + optional KMS-based envelope encryption). This ticket coordinates cross-team acceptance criteria and sign-offs required before the toggle ships: engineering readiness for migration hooks and rollout flags; des...
text: PRD: Add a user-facing toggle in the Redwood Console to enable the Private VPC upgrade path (v2 networking + optional KMS-based envelope encryption). This ticket coordinates cross-team acceptance criteria and sign-offs required before the toggle ships: engineering readiness for migration hooks and rollout flags; design acceptance for console UX, error states, and migration prompts; legal/compliance confirmation for data residency and export control language. Scope clarifications added after initial PRD: KMS integration will be optional for first wave; migration rollback path must be automated; customers with Reserved Dedicated instances are out of scope for this rollout. Goal: produce a single acceptance matrix and checklist that individual teams can sign off against to unblock release-2026.03.

title: Reservation handshake runbook and scaling safety net
preview: Create an end-to-end runbook and negotiation flow for dedicated capacity reservations that: 1) standardizes the reservation handshake between Sales/CS/Provisioning, 2) encodes throughput guarantees into deployable plans, and 3) provides a safety net of autoscale and burst mechanisms to protect customer SLAs while pr...
text: Create an end-to-end runbook and negotiation flow for dedicated capacity reservations that: 1) standardizes the reservation handshake between Sales/CS/Provisioning, 2) encodes throughput guarantees into deployable plans, and 3) provides a safety net of autoscale and burst mechanisms to protect customer SLAs while preserving cluster stability and cost controls. This artifact should be the canonical source for onboarding engineers, SREs, and account teams when a customer requests committed throughput or reserved GPU pools.

title: Quantile Warranty & Autoscale Coordination for Dedicated Serve Capacity
preview: Problem statement: enterprise customers need clear, measurable guarantees for dedicated GPU pools. Current SLA drafts use single-point targets (e.g., tokens/min) and simple credit tables that don't reflect bursty real-world traffic or staggered degradation. This ticket defines a quantile-driven warranty (p95/p99 win...
text:
Problem statement: enterprise customers need clear, measurable guarantees for dedicated GPU pools. Current SLA drafts use single-point targets (e.g., tokens/min) and simple credit tables that don't reflect bursty real-world traffic or staggered degradation. This ticket defines a quantile-driven warranty (p95/p99 windows + AUC degradation) and ties it to autoscaler interlocks, customer notices, and contract crediting language.

Scope:
- Define measurable SLA metrics (quantiles, sustained shortfalls, AUC-based crediting).
- Specify autoscaler guardrails and interlock logic to avoid oscillation between autoscale and remediation credits.
- Draft customer-facing contract clauses (notice windows, remediation steps, credit computation appendix).
- Produce monitoring dashboards and alerting thresholds needed to support legal measurement.

Out of scope: per-tenant billing/credits plumbing and ...

title: Inference Moat Metric: quantifying serving advantage & operational readiness
preview: Problem: Sales, product and exec teams lack a single, quantitative view of Redwood's 'inference moat' — the concrete serving, routing, and ops capabilities buyers cite when choosing us over other providers. Existing artifacts are fragmented (benchmarks, PRDs, ad-hoc research). This ticket will synthesize a repeatabl...
text: Problem: Sales, product and exec teams lack a single, quantitative view of Redwood's 'inference moat' — the concrete serving, routing, and ops capabilities buyers cite when choosing us over other providers. Existing artifacts are fragmented (benchmarks, PRDs, ad-hoc research). This ticket will synthesize a repeatable metric ("Inference Moat Score") and an operations-ready signal set that can be surfaced in Console and exported to GTM. The goal is to turn qualitative win-loss themes into measurable signals that drive roadmap prioritization, pricing, and competitive messaging.

title: Privilege shadowing, SSO assertion hardening, and KMS audit sign-off
preview: Clarify requirements, scope changes, and acceptance criteria for three tightly-coupled security controls for Private/VPC deployments: (1) privilege shadowing (temporary operator impersonation with auditable traces), (2) SSO/SAML assertion hardening and federation verification, and (3) KMS/HSM-integrated audit eviden...
text: Clarify requirements, scope changes, and acceptance criteria for three tightly-coupled security controls for Private/VPC deployments: (1) privilege shadowing (temporary operator impersonation with auditable traces), (2) SSO/SAML assertion hardening and federation verification, and (3) KMS/HSM-integrated audit evidence and key-escrow operational controls. This ticket consolidates ambiguous PRD language, captures engineering constraints, and presents a sign-off checklist for security, legal, and product owners.

title: Emergent LLM Pulse: Rapid Recon & Procurement Trigger Playbook
preview: Create a repeatable, lightweight process (Emergent LLM Pulse) for rapidly identifying promising new open / vendor LLMs, scoring them for deployment fit, and emitting automated procurement/trial triggers for Redwood teams. The output will be a taxonomy, telemetry-driven alert rules, a one-page procurement decision ca...
text: Create a repeatable, lightweight process (Emergent LLM Pulse) for rapidly identifying promising new open / vendor LLMs, scoring them for deployment fit, and emitting automated procurement/trial triggers for Redwood teams. The output will be a taxonomy, telemetry-driven alert rules, a one-page procurement decision canvas, and a prototype dashboard that surfaces top emergent models and suggested fallback/rollout actions.

title: Cross-Product Launch Calendar: Dynamic Release Train Orchestrator
preview: Objective: Build a Dynamic Release Train Orchestrator (DRTO) that provides a single authoritative launch calendar and automated conflict-resolution layer for runtime, console, and API releases. DRTO should allow teams to reserve launch slots, attach canary windows, declare blackout windows and capacity reservations,...
text:
Objective: Build a Dynamic Release Train Orchestrator (DRTO) that provides a single authoritative launch calendar and automated conflict-resolution layer for runtime, console, and API releases. DRTO should allow teams to reserve launch slots, attach canary windows, declare blackout windows and capacity reservations, and automatically compute safe stagger policies and fallback lanes when conflicts are detected.

Background: Launch friction has been a recurring source of latency and outages when multiple product teams schedule large runtime or console changes in overlapping windows. The current playbooks are manual (spreadsheets + Slack) and cause avoidable collisions, overbooked GPU capacity, and late-stage rollbacks. This initiative aims to reduce schedule conflicts, accelerate cross-team coordination, and provide automated signals for go/no-go decisions.

Scope (MVP):
- Central calen...

title: initiative-level-staffing-buffer-policy-and-governance
preview: Background: As we scale Redwoods product footprint and expand Dedicated/Private offerings, cycle plans are encountering recurring over-commitment and churn driven by reactive engineering requests, infra incidents, and late-requested compliance work. Current ad-hoc buffers (team-level fudge factors) lead to inconsist...
text:
Background: As we scale Redwoods product footprint and expand Dedicated/Private offerings, cycle plans are encountering recurring over-commitment and churn driven by reactive engineering requests, infra incidents, and late-requested compliance work. Current ad-hoc buffers (team-level fudge factors) lead to inconsistent treatment across initiatives and surprise scope cuts mid-cycle.

Goal: Define a repeatable, initiative-level staffing buffer policy and lightweight governance process that: 1) reduces mid-cycle scope volatility, 2) aligns expectations between PM/Eng/Design, and 3) preserves runway for high-likelihood emergent work. Policy should be actionable in planning workflows and instrumented in the Cycle Capacity Simulator.

Proposed policy (summary):
- Buffer sizing: baseline buffer = max(10% of raw estimated effort, 1 velocity point per 2 engineers) + risk-adjustment band (0%/5%...

title: PRD: Temporal Credit Faucet & Rapid Burst Reconciler for Dedicated Capacity
preview: Problem statement: Customers using Dedicated capacity report two recurring pain points: (1) short-lived traffic spikes cause failed requests because reserved pools are fully utilized and prewarm latency for new workers is high, and (2) tenant isolation is brittle when burst policies allow lateral preemption or aggre...
text: Problem statement: Customers using Dedicated capacity report two recurring pain points: (1) short-lived traffic spikes cause failed requests because reserved pools are fully utilized and prewarm latency for new workers is high, and (2) tenant isolation is brittle when burst policies allow lateral preemption or aggressive backfill that impacts SLA-critical tenants. Goal: Design a system-level PRD that introduces a temporal credit faucet (small, time-limited emergency credits) plus a rapid burst reconciler (fast backfill + non-disruptive prewarm handoff) to reduce 5xx rate during spikes and preserve SLAs for priority tenants. This PRD covers API surface, console UX, autoscale orchestration behavior, observability, rollout strategy, and acceptance criteria.

title: OKR Horizon Snapshot & Decision Tree for Quarterly Capacity Allocation
preview: Goal: produce an operational playbook and an evidence-backed decision tree to convert high-level Company OKRs into a prioritized set of cross-functional initiatives and concrete capacity allocations for the quarter. Scope: consolidates inputs from GTM, Engineering, Platform, and Research; defines a 3-axis scoring ru...
text: Goal: produce an operational playbook and an evidence-backed decision tree to convert high-level Company OKRs into a prioritized set of cross-functional initiatives and concrete capacity allocations for the quarter. Scope: consolidates inputs from GTM, Engineering, Platform, and Research; defines a 3-axis scoring rubric (impact, cost-to-implement, execution-risk); outputs a ranked initiative list, hedged capacity plan (reserved/burst/hosted mixes), and escalation lanes for resource conflicts. Deliverables: 1) OKR Horizon Snapshot PDF, 2) Decision Tree workbook (sheet + scoring), 3) Runbook for quarterly rebalancing and emergency reallocation, 4) Guardrails for Dedicated vs Hosted trade-offs.

title: Modular retention templates and automated evidence playbook for request data
preview: Goal: Provide a modular, policy-driven retention template system for prompts/responses/request logs/metrics plus a standardized automated evidence playbook that can produce compliance-ready export bundles for audits. The feature should make it simple for customers to pick or customize retention profiles (examples: G...
text:
Goal: Provide a modular, policy-driven retention template system for prompts/responses/request logs/metrics plus a standardized automated evidence playbook that can produce compliance-ready export bundles for audits. The feature should make it simple for customers to pick or customize retention profiles (examples: GDPR-Short, HIPAA-Long, EU-Residency-Only), visualize effective retention per route/tenant, and produce signed evidence packages with metadata and provenance for auditors.

Scope:
- Authoring UI in Console for 'Retention Templates' (create, clone, compose).
- Template primitives: data classes (prompts, responses, request-metadata, logs, metrics), retention TTL, residency constraints, anonymization/transformation rules, and evidence rules (export cadence, bundle format, signatures).
- Template inheritance: org -> project -> route overrides with clear effective retention resol...

title: Automated eval triggers for catalog and customer-locked model upgrades
preview: Goal: implement automated evaluation triggers that run targeted quality and safety suites whenever a model version is pinned, unpinned, or upgraded — for both catalog-managed models and customer-locked (pinned) models. This ticket defines the product scope, acceptance criteria, rollout policy, and required guardrail...
text:
Goal: implement automated evaluation triggers that run targeted quality and safety suites whenever a model version is pinned, unpinned, or upgraded — for both catalog-managed models and customer-locked (pinned) models. This ticket defines the product scope, acceptance criteria, rollout policy, and required guardrails for the feature.

Why: We continue to see manual pin upgrades cause regressions in customer apps. Running lightweight, focused evals as a preflight and post-deploy step will reduce undetected regressions and enable safer rollouts. The system must be configurable (which suites to run), cost-aware (avoid runaway eval costs), and actionable (clear pass/fail gating and rollback recommendations).

Scope:
- Trigger points: catalog model version changes, customer pin upgrades, config diffs that affect runtime (quantization, tokenization, prompts-as-config).
- Eval types: smoke (...

title: Rolling capacity triage guardrails and scope reserve
preview:
Background:
Redwood's growth and increasing enterprise demand are stretching cycle capacity. Teams report mid-cycle churn from unplanned incidents, urgent customer escalations, and cross-functional rollouts which erode committed scope. We need standard, repeatable guardrails for assigning a rolling scope reserve and...
text:
Background:
Redwood's growth and increasing enterprise demand are stretching cycle capacity. Teams report mid-cycle churn from unplanned incidents, urgent customer escalations, and cross-functional rollouts which erode committed scope. We need standard, repeatable guardrails for assigning a rolling scope reserve and triaging mid-cycle work so that delivery predictability improves without blocking critical incidents.

Goal:
Define a lightweight, repeatable process and decision rubric that: 1) allocates a configurable reserve of team capacity each cycle; 2) standardizes triage steps for mid-cycle requests (feature changes, production incidents, partner needs); 3) provides explicit rules for scope cuts and redeployment of reserves during surges.

Success metrics:
- Cycle commitment hit rate (stories completed / stories committed) increases by 20% within two cycles after rollout.
- Mean u...

title: Release documentation orchestration and stakeholder traffic control
preview: Objective: Produce coordinated, audience-tailored release artifacts (customer-facing changelog, admin changelog, internal one-pager, and exec summary) and orchestrate an efficient, auditable stakeholder review flow so the release communications can ship on the planned date without last-minute redactions or complianc...
text:
Objective: Produce coordinated, audience-tailored release artifacts (customer-facing changelog, admin changelog, internal one-pager, and exec summary) and orchestrate an efficient, auditable stakeholder review flow so the release communications can ship on the planned date without last-minute redactions or compliance issues.

Scope:
- Draft two customer-facing changelogs: 'Consumer' (short, benefits-focused) and 'Admin' (detailed breaking changes, migration steps, RBAC/KMS notes).
- Create an internal release brief for support, sales, and account teams including rollout schedule, anticipated tickets, and escalation contacts.
- Produce an executive summary (1-pager) for leadership and post-release metrics owners.
- Run a cross-functional signoff loop with Engineering, Design, Security, Legal, Docs, Solutions Eng, and GTM.
- Localize customer-facing changelogs for EN, FR, DE; queue to t...

title: Security readiness gates and crypto control matrix for Private VPC deployment
preview: PRD addendum that consolidates security and compliance acceptance gates specifically for Private (VPC / on-prem) deployments. Focus areas: SSO/SAML and SCIM provisioning, RBAC role model and enforcement, tamper-evident audit logging and retention, and customer keying via KMS/HSM. Establish a control matrix mapping r...
text: PRD addendum that consolidates security and compliance acceptance gates specifically for Private (VPC / on-prem) deployments. Focus areas: SSO/SAML and SCIM provisioning, RBAC role model and enforcement, tamper-evident audit logging and retention, and customer keying via KMS/HSM. Establish a control matrix mapping requirement -> owner -> verification method -> sign-off authority, and include explicit rollback/fallback procedures and scope-change triggers that require re-certification.

title: Decoupled Reserved + Burst Pricing Pilot for Dedicated GPU Capacity
preview: Objective: run a structured pricing and packaging experiment for Dedicated capacity that separates baseline reserved GPU commitments from short-term burst credits and autoscale tokens, and validate SLA tier willingness-to-pay among enterprise prospects. Deliverables: experiment matrix, pilot contracts for 3 customer...
text: Objective: run a structured pricing and packaging experiment for Dedicated capacity that separates baseline reserved GPU commitments from short-term burst credits and autoscale tokens, and validate SLA tier willingness-to-pay among enterprise prospects. Deliverables: experiment matrix, pilot contracts for 3 customers, instrumentation dashboard for unit economics, legal playbook for contract/security questionnaire answers, and rollout recommendation.

title: Scheduled tenant-scoped compliance bundles with residency-aware delivery
preview:
Problem: Enterprise customers (SOC2/ISO-regulated) need scheduled, verifiable evidence bundles (PDF/CSV) that respect tenant data residency and retention policies. Current ad-hoc exports from the console are manual, region-agnostic, and do not include signed audit manifests or automatic retention-based pruning.

Sco...
text:
Problem: Enterprise customers (SOC2/ISO-regulated) need scheduled, verifiable evidence bundles (PDF/CSV) that respect tenant data residency and retention policies. Current ad-hoc exports from the console are manual, region-agnostic, and do not include signed audit manifests or automatic retention-based pruning.

Scope: Provide a first-class scheduled export feature for the Console that produces time-boxed compliance bundles per tenant. Bundles must: (1) be scoped to tenant(s) and their residency setting, (2) obey retention windows (exclude records beyond tenant retention), (3) include a signed manifest (SHA256 checksums) and optional PGP signing, (4) support PDF summary + CSV raw exports, (5) be deliverable via region-aware S3 endpoints or secure download links, and (6) surface status in a new Compliance Exports dashboard with run history, success/failure reasons, and replay.

Out of ...

title: Billing intake lifecycle and decision heuristics for enterprise pricing requests
preview: Purpose: Define a repeatable intake-to-resolution lifecycle and a decision heuristic for non-bug customer requests about pricing, commitments, invoicing, cost allocation, and marketplace billing. This ticket captures the operational playbook that will be used by Sales/CS/Early Customers, Growth, and Product to triag...
text: Purpose: Define a repeatable intake-to-resolution lifecycle and a decision heuristic for non-bug customer requests about pricing, commitments, invoicing, cost allocation, and marketplace billing. This ticket captures the operational playbook that will be used by Sales/CS/Early Customers, Growth, and Product to triage and route requests so we can: 1) reduce time-to-decision, 2) apply consistent commercial outcomes, and 3) surface engineering cost and legal constraints early.

title: Stability Fingerprint & Actionable Observability for Model Rollouts
preview: Build a 'Stability Fingerprint' experience in Console that surfaces per-cohort health and causality signals for canary rollouts, A/B tests, and shadow traffic. The feature bundles cohort-level dashboards, side-by-side metric diffs, trace links, per-route distributions (latency, p99, error rates), and automated recom...
text: Build a 'Stability Fingerprint' experience in Console that surfaces per-cohort health and causality signals for canary rollouts, A/B tests, and shadow traffic. The feature bundles cohort-level dashboards, side-by-side metric diffs, trace links, per-route distributions (latency, p99, error rates), and automated recommendations for safe actions (pause, ramp, rollback). It should integrate with the rollout policy engine to enable threshold-based auto-rollback and dry-run modes, and provide explainable signal-attribution so engineers can quickly determine whether regressions are model-quality, infra, or traffic-related.

title: Field enablement kit: objection-flow maps, demo prompts, rollback playbooks
preview: Create a compact, field-ready enablement kit for Sales and CS to surface runtime-1.20 features (Latency-Aware Routing, adaptive prefix caching improvements, and canary rollout controls). Deliverables: concise battlecard (2-page), objection-response flow maps, 5 scenario demo scripts (API+Console), quick rollback/run...
text: Create a compact, field-ready enablement kit for Sales and CS to surface runtime-1.20 features (Latency-Aware Routing, adaptive prefix caching improvements, and canary rollout controls). Deliverables: concise battlecard (2-page), objection-response flow maps, 5 scenario demo scripts (API+Console), quick rollback/runbook snippets for common incidents, and a coordinated release-notes summary for CS Slack and release email. Coordinate review cycles with Engineering, Support, Legal, and Docs.

title: Cohort Storyboard + Rollback Policy Editor for rollout observability
preview: Problem: Engineers and SREs running canary/A-B/ shadow rollouts lack a single, narrative-driven view that ties per-route metrics, request traces, and cohort diffs together with an editable auto-rollback policy. Current Console panels surface metric tables and simple rollouts, but teams spend manual effort correlatin...
text:
Problem: Engineers and SREs running canary/A-B/ shadow rollouts lack a single, narrative-driven view that ties per-route metrics, request traces, and cohort diffs together with an editable auto-rollback policy. Current Console panels surface metric tables and simple rollouts, but teams spend manual effort correlating trace samples, error fingerprints, and traffic-slice differences before deciding to roll back.

Goal: Build a "Cohort Storyboard" in Console — a timeline-first, side-by-side comparison that links cohorts to sample traces, per-route token/latency/cost breakdowns, and a visual rollback policy editor (thresholds, hysteresis, and staged actions). The feature should also enable hypothesis annotations and exportable snapshots for postmortems.

Scope: MVP focuses on HTTP/text-generation routes and the most common rollout types: canary (percent-based), A/B (split), and shadow. In...

title: Custom SLA Profiles + Eval-Gated Cutover Playbook for Hosted Endpoints
preview: Define and deliver customizable per-tenant SLA profiles for hosted chat/completions/embeddings/rerank endpoints, gated by eval-driven telemetry and an automated cutover playbook that supports partial rollbacks and safe fallbacks.
text: Define and deliver customizable per-tenant SLA profiles for hosted chat/completions/embeddings/rerank endpoints, gated by eval-driven telemetry and an automated cutover playbook that supports partial rollbacks and safe fallbacks.
